claim: For laminar incompressible pipe flow, pressure drop scales as viscosity times length times mean velocity divided by radius squared.
success_criteria: Uses or derives Delta P = 8 mu L V / r^2 or an equivalent Hagen-Poiseuille relation.
Performs dimensional consistency or symbolic proportionality check.
failure_modes: Uses turbulent Darcy-Weisbach formula without reconciling assumptions.
prompt: For incompressible laminar pipe flow, test whether pressure drop is proportional to viscosity, length, and mean velocity, and inversely proportional to radius squared under Hagen-Poiseuille assumptions.
